title: Sphaerocionium nitidulum
taxonomy: Sphaerocionium nitidulum belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, also known as the hauouriopsida, within the division Pteridophyta. This classification is further categorized into the family Polypodiaceae. The genus Sphaerocionium is a group of ferns that are generally epiphytic. Sphaerocionium nitidulum specifically is a species of fern within this genus. Its classification is still being studied and refined by botanists.

intro: Sphaerocionium nitidulum, also known as the shiny jellyfish lichen, is a type of foliose lichen, a composite organism consisting of fungi and algae that grow together in a symbiotic relationship. The health benefits of Sphaerocionium nitidulum include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoids, such as usnic acid, and depsides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Sphaerocionium nitidulum is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections.
